summary: All five findings are resolved. The fixes correctly move notification permission into a one-shot user gesture, contain badge promise rejections, hide stale card alerts, implement true set equality, and correct the reactor lifecycle documentation. Full typechecking passes, and the 34 targeted tests pass.

findings.issue: The notification permission request now runs synchronously from the first pointer or keyboard gesture, with both listeners removed immediately and again during cleanup.
Both badge operations now route rejected promises to a warning instead of causing unhandled rejections.
The card alert indicator is now gated on a connected entry, so stale retained values are not presented as live alerts.
The comparator now performs genuine set equality using unique-set sizes and membership, including for duplicate-containing inputs.
Both comments now accurately state that scan subscribes synchronously during construction and that the emitter is cleared on scan/source teardown.

responseToRebuttal: F1: Conceded and resolved. The explicit settings control was a suggested implementation, not an independent requirement; the one-shot pointerdown/keydown path fixes the actual user-activation defect and has correct teardown. F2: Resolved; optional API absence remains a no-op while promise failures are contained. F3: Resolved; the connected-state gate aligns the card with the badge’s live-only semantics. F4: Resolved; the new predicate is true set equality. F5: Resolved; both lifecycle comments now match the pinned reactor implementation. I also accept the environment explanation: the prior full-suite EROFS failures were sandbox artifacts and the unchanged process-stream failure was not held as a finding.
